data.jd_text: About Valtech At Valtech, we are reshaping the way brands connect with their customers. As a global digital innovation company, we fuse creativity, technology, and strategy to solve complex business challenges and deliver exceptional experiences. With over 6,500 experts in 60+ offices across 20+ countries, we are expanding our footprint in MENA with Dubai as the strategic hub. At Valtech we thrive on driving business transformation as trusted partners to the world’s most recognized brands. To our people we offer growth opportunities, a values-driven culture, international careers and the chance to shape the future of experience. About The Team We are part of Valtech MENA, the experience innovation company, combining the uncommon to unlock a better way for people to experience the world. We work at the intersection of disciplines, cultures, and perspectives to create solutions that go beyond expectations, helping brands move ahead and lead in a digital world. We value clarity, collaboration, and a strong respect for craft. That mindset shapes both what we create and how we work together. Overview We are looking for an Experience Operations Lead to take ownership of how experience design is delivered across projects. This role focuses on turning strong experience direction into high quality, well structured delivery. You will ensure that ideas hold up through the realities of timelines, tools, team dynamics, and technical constraints, without losing clarity or intent. You will define how delivery works, support teams in executing it, and ensure a consistent standard across all work. The role You are responsible for making sure that experience design is delivered properly, consistently, and at a high level of quality. You work from a clear experience direction and help shape how it is delivered in practice. You are not solely responsible for defining the vision, but you contribute to how it evolves through real delivery. You create clarity where there is complexity and structure where there is ambiguity. You care about craft and about the systems that allow that craft to scale. data.title: Director – Infrastructure Operations
data.jd_text: Established in 2004, Dubai Holding is a global investment company with investments in more than 34 countries and a combined workforce of nearly 45,000 individuals. In line with the vision of Dubai’s leadership and economic diversification strategy, Dubai Holding companies have nurtured sectors, irrevocably transforming Dubai’s socio-economic landscape and positioning Dubai as a diversified, globally integrated economy. Dubai Holding is committed to the diversification of Dubai’s non-oil economy. Our portfolio, valued at over AED 284+ billion, spans 10 sectors, including real estate, hospitality, leisure & entertainment, ICT, design, education, media, retail, manufacturing & logistics, and science. For the Good of Tomorrow Dubai Holding is looking to hire a Director - Infrastructure Operations reporting to the Vice President - Cloud, Infrastructure and Workplace Services. This is a senior leadership role accountable for the design, delivery, and continuous evolution of Dubai Holding's infrastructure services portfolio, spanning compute, storage, data centre, network, and Microsoft 365 environments. The role holds end-to-end ownership of infrastructure operations across the organisation, orchestrating the combined capabilities of internal teams, strategic third-party partners, and emerging technologies to deliver resilient, secure, and high-performing services at scale. A core contributor to the group-wide infrastructure strategy and technology roadmap, the Director plays an integral role in shaping and executing the direction of Dubai Holding's infrastructure function — ensuring all solutions and initiatives are grounded in sound architectural principles, aligned to security and audit requirements, and structured to deliver tangible business value. The Director is a change agent who thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. The role brings together strategic vision and operational rigour to drive technology modernisation, elevate service quality, enhance customer satisfaction, and achieve sustainable operational efficiency across the organisation. If you are ready to embark on a dynamic journey where your talents are valued, and your contributions make a real impact, we would like to hear from you. Key Responsibilities: Define and own the organisation's infrastructure architecture strategy, ensuring solutions are scalable, resilient, and secure. Partner with cross-functional teams and business verticals to design and deliver infrastructure that meets both current demands and future growth requirements. Take executive ownership of the availability, stability, recoverability, and performance of all infrastructure services across the organisation, ensuring they remain cost-optimised, fit for purpose, and aligned with agreed service levels. data.title: Director of Retail Operations
data.jd_text: We are a leading multinational retailer based on GCC. We are looking for an experienced Director of Retail Operations to organize and oversee the operations of our stores. You will assume responsibility for the company’s overall success by setting targets, supervising managers, and ensuring that they are attained. The Operations Director is expected to execute company strategies by maximizing store profitability. You will have control over store expenses, protect company assets, and manage overall store standards and processes for customer service excellence, merchandising and inventory management, retail operations, and ensure that team participation is consistent and well managed. Effective operations directors are skilled in managing diverse operations. They have a strategic mindset and are excellent leaders. Excellent financial and operational planning abilities are essential for this role. The goal is to ensure that our company meets and exceeds the expectations of business development and efficiency. Duties and responsibilities Provides leadership to the team and oversees retail activities. Hires, trains, and oversees management team performance. Full P/L responsibility. Sets objectives for retail teams to ensure a high standard of service provided in the store. Ensures structure is provided for the retail teams in KPIs, policies and procedures, trading feedback processes, trading calendars, and controlling retail costs. Reviews and analyzes sales and operational records and reports; uses data to project sales, determines profitability and targets, and identifies potential new markets. Prepares and implements the county budget; monitors and approves expenses. Collaborates with managers to develop sales goals for each location, monitors progress toward those goals and develops strategies to attract and retain customers. Develops marketing programs and promotions to increase sales and/or introduce new products. Identifies opportunities for improvement; designs and implements training, strategies, policies, goals, and other resources to maximize productivity and morale. Schedules and directs regular sales meetings and events to share information, set and revise goals, and increase morale. Oversees warehouse operations in conjunction with the Supply Chain Manager. Manages inventory by monitoring inventory levels and planning stock supply to ensure the availability of the full line of products. Maintains knowledge of the market, competition, and best practices and trends in sales techniques and strategies. Conducts performance evaluations that are timely and constructive. Handles discipline and termination of employees in accordance with company policy and labor law. Performs other duties as assigned. Requirements Strong verbal and written communication skills. Excellent sales and customer service skills, with proven negotiation skills. Excellent math skills to draft and implement budgets and conduct data analysis. Excellent supervisory and lead

data.jd_text: A confidential, customer-focused organisation is seeking a Head of Digital Product Delivery (Mobile App) to lead the strategy, prioritisation, and delivery of a flagship consumer mobile app and its associated customer-facing digital product capabilities. This is a senior, business-led role with end-to-end accountability for turning strategic ambition into tangible digital outcomes. The role is intentionally hands-on designed for a leader who combines strong product thinking with disciplined programme and delivery execution, and who uses modern delivery practices to ship outcomes. The Role As Head of Digital Product Delivery (Mobile App) , you will act as the senior business owner for the mobile app. You will define what the product is, how it evolves, and how it delivers value for customers and the business, working in close partnership with Digital Technology teams and external delivery partners. While not responsible for hands-on software development, this role is deeply embedded in product, programme, and delivery leadership. You will personally drive planning, prioritisation, sequencing, dependencies, milestones, and day-to-day execution to ensure initiatives are delivered with quality, pace, and measurable impact. data.title: Head of Network Infrastructure Operation
data.jd_text: The Head of Network Infrastructure Operations plays a critical role in overseeing the strategic direction of network infrastructure, IT programs, and operational initiatives, ensuring alignment with the organization’s overarching goals. This role is responsible for ensuring that network and IT initiatives deliver strategic value while supporting continuous improvement to enhance operational efficiency and effectiveness. Operational Excellence & NOC Management Oversee the 24/7 Network Operations Center (NOC) to ensure proactive monitoring. Establish and monitor critical SLAs/SLOs for network uptime and performance . Lead engineering teams through high-pressure, live operational environments and time-sensitive event windows . Collaborate with non-technical event organizers to translate business needs into technical network specifications. Lead high-severity incident response and conduct detailed Root Cause Analysis (RCA) . Strategic Infrastructure Roadmap Design and implement scalable network architectures (LAN/WAN, Data Center, Cloud) . Evaluate emerging technologies such as SD-WAN, WiFi 6E, and SASE to maintain a competitive edge. Direct the decommissioning of legacy hardware and migration to modern infrastructure stacks . Network Automation & Innovation Drive initiatives to automate routine configurations . Implement automated CI/CD pipelines for network changes . Standardize network deployments using tools such as Ansible, Terraform, or Python . Risk Management & Security Compliance Partner with Security teams to enforce Zero Trust models and robust firewall policies . Ensure all network assets comply with global standards such as ISO 27001 and SOC2 . Manage disaster recovery (DR) planning and regular business continuity testing . data.title: Chief Executive Officer
data.jd_text: Our Client Our client is a fast-growing hospitality and F&B group with a diversified portfolio of restaurant concepts and lifestyle venues across the UAE. The group is backed by strong investors and is entering a disciplined scale-up phase. The Role The Chief Executive Officer will lead the groups overall strategy, performance, and expansion agenda across existing and new concepts. This role will have full P&L responsibility and oversight of operations, finance, brand, and growth. A key focus will be standardising operations, strengthening leadership depth, and ensuring profitability across the portfolio. The CEO will work closely with shareholders on expansion strategy, franchising, and regional growth. The role will also act as the public face of the group with partners, landlords, and regulators. Candidate Profile Candidates must have senior executive experience within hospitality, F&B, or multi-brand consumer environments. Strong exposure to scaling restaurant or lifestyle concepts is essential. Experience operating in the UAE market is highly preferred. The ideal candidate combines commercial discipline with brand sensitivity and people leadership. Board and investor-facing experience is a strong advantage.

data.title: Vice President and Head of Program Lending - Business Banking Group
data.jd_text: About the Role: As Head of Program Lending within the Business Banking Group, you’ll lead the design, development, and execution of scalable, risk-aligned credit programs tailored for SME customers. This role is pivotal in driving financial inclusion, enabling credit growth, and expanding RAKBANK’s reach across diverse SME segments. You’ll work cross-functionally to build lending solutions that are commercially viable, digitally enabled, and compliant with regulatory standards, and report directly to the Head of Business Banking Products. What You’ll Be Doing: Define and execute the program lending strategy aligned with business growth and risk appetite. Identify SME segments and design sector-specific credit programs (e.g., supplier finance, microfinance). Build partnerships with anchor corporates, fintechs, and government agencies. Collaborate with Credit, Risk, Data, and Tech teams to build underwriting and eligibility models. Digitize end-to-end lending journeys from onboarding to servicing. Integrate lending solutions into digital platforms and servicing channels. Own lending targets, portfolio quality, and risk-adjusted returns. Monitor KPIs including approval rates, disbursement speed, delinquency, and recovery ratios. Optimize program mix across risk segments to balance growth and profitability. Design frictionless, customer-friendly credit journeys. Embed flexible repayment features and lifecycle lending options. Collaborate with CX and distribution teams to enhance onboarding and servicing. Lead a multidisciplinary team of program managers and analysts. Champion cross-functional collaboration and agile execution. Ensure compliance with internal and regulatory frameworks (CBUAE, AML/CFT). What We’re Looking For: A strategic thinker with deep expertise in SME lending and program-based finance. Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams and deliver complex product roadmaps. Strong commercial acumen with a track record of driving profitability and innovation. Experience navigating regulatory frameworks and risk governance structures. A collaborative leader who thrives in matrixed environments and agile squads. Skills: Deep knowledge of SME banking, credit dynamics, and alternative underwriting models. Expertise in lending products: term loans, working capital, invoice financing, government-backed schemes. Familiarity with digital ecosystems and integration of lending into broader platforms. Skilled in credit program structuring, risk-return optimization, and data-driven decisioning.Visionary leadership with strong execution capabilities. Influencing skills across senior stakeholders and external partners. Resilience, adaptability, and a growth mindset. Commitment to continuous improvement, innovation, and team development. What You’ll Gain: Competitive compensation and performance-based incentives Medical coverage for you and your family Comprehensive life insurance Exposure to high-impact strategic projects Supportive and inclusive work
